title: Mitochondria Function
definition: The role of mitochondria in generating cellular energy through oxidative phosphorylation.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Mitochondria are often referred to as the powerhouses of the cell because they produce most of a cell's energy in the form of ATP.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Mitochondria are essential organelles that generate energy for cells through oxidative phosphorylation, playing a key role in various physiological processes.
difficulty_levels.expert: An exploration of the complex biochemistry and physics underlying mitochondrial function, offering insights into aging, metabolic disorders, and potential applications like anti-aging research and mitochondrial replacement therapy.

extra_fields.insight: The intricate inner workings of the powerhouses that fuel life at a cellular level.
